title: Sheep footbath design and use
subtitle: Building and running a footbath as part of routine flock foot health.
excerpt: A footbath is the most efficient way to treat the whole flock's feet at once, either as a routine preventive or as part of a foot-rot treatment programme. The bath needs to be long enough, deep enough, and positioned so the sheep can't avoid walking through it. Here's how to build one and how to use it.
sourceNotes: AHDB Beef and Lamb, Five-Point Plan for Sheep Foot Health. DEFRA/APHA Sheep Welfare Code. SAC Consulting Scotland, Sheep Lameness Management.

glossaryTerms.definition: A water-soluble mineral compound used as a 10% footbath solution to harden hooves and reduce the incidence of foot rot and scald.
A shallow trough containing a disinfectant or mineral solution through which sheep are walked to treat or prevent foot disease.
A contagious bacterial infection of the hoof caused by Dichelobacter nodosus, causing separation of the hoof wall and severe lameness.

content.text: A
footbath
as part of a routine foot health programme reduces the incidence of
foot rot

and foot scald over time by hardening the hoof wall and reducing bacterial load between the digits. Used after paring, it also helps healing. It's most useful as part of a wider programme, footbathing alone, without the other elements of good foot management, has limited effect.
Footbath dimensions

The bath needs to be: at least 3 m long (so each sheep takes at least three steps through it), at least 50 cm wide (one sheep width), and 10 cm deep. A shorter bath means sheep leap through without their feet spending enough time in the solution.
Construction options
A purpose-built concrete trough sited within the race, permanent, easy to fill and drain
A heavy-duty rubber trough placed in the race, portable, cheap, washes out easily
Sections of old corrugated roofing sheet sealed with silicone, low-cost and functional for a small flock
What solution to use
For routine prevention: 10%
zinc sulphate

solution, 10 kg of zinc sulphate crystals dissolved in 100 litres of water. This is the safest option for repeated use. Allow the sheep to stand in the solution for two to three minutes where possible rather than simply walking through.
For active foot rot treatment, your vet may prescribe an antibiotic footbath solution. Follow the prescription precisely, over-concentration causes chemical burns on the skin between the digits.
Running the flock through

Run the flock through the race and the bath before turning them out onto clean, dry ground. Running them through onto mud immediately after reduces the treatment time the solution is in contact with the foot.
Change the solution after 200,300 sheep have passed through, or when visibly dirty. Heavily contaminated solution carries the infection from sheep to sheep rather than treating it.
Dispose of used footbath solution according to the product label. Zinc sulphate and formalin solutions should not be disposed of in watercourses.
